title: CleanSpark Delivers $181 Million in Q1 Revenue, Strengthens Balance Sheet, and Advances Multi-Gigawatt AI Infrastructure Platform
text: CleanSpark reported $181.2 million in quarterly revenues for the three months ended December 31, 2025, representing an 11.6% increase from the prior year's $162.3 million. However, the company posted a net loss of $378.7 million, or $1.35 per share, compared to net income of $246.8 million in the same prior period. The company secured up to 890 megawatts of new utility-grade power capacity in the Houston region and acquired an additional 122-acre parcel at its Sandersville site to advance toward AI tenancy. CEO Matt Schultz stated: CleanSpark exited the quarter with one of the strongest balance sheets in our sector. As of December 31, 2025, CleanSpark maintained $458.1 million in cash, $1.0 billion in Bitcoin holdings, $1.3 billion in working capital, total assets of $3.3 billion, and total stockholders' equity of $1.4 billion. The company is transitioning from a single-track bitcoin mining operation toward a multi-revenue infrastructure platform. President and CFO Gary Vecchiarelli emphasized: Bitcoin mining generates the cash flow, AI infrastructure monetizes assets over the long term.
